Course Title: AI in Industrial Control Systems (ICS): New Risks
Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Plant managers, operations supervisors, facility engineers, OT technicians, industrial automation specialists, process control engineers, safety managers, maintenance supervisors
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Engineering, Facilities Management, Safety, IT/OT Security, Industrial Automation
Description: How AI introduces new attack surfaces in operational technology.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with industrial or operational environments (such as manufacturing, energy, or agriculture) and a general awareness of cybersecurity concepts will help you get the most from this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways in which AI introduces new attack surfaces within Industrial Control Systems (ICS) across sectors such as agriculture, energy, and mining. You will also be able to explain the potential risks these vulnerabilities pose to operational technology and apply foundational strategies to recognise and respond to AI-related threats in your working environment.
